title: carex fusca subsp. intricata
intro: Carex fusca subsp. intricata, also known as the lesser wood sedge, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Cyperaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The lesser wood sedge is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential interactions.
